posts.text:
BREAKING: President Trump is preparing to invoke Cold War-era powers to renew oil production off the southern California coast, per Bloomberg.

Trump is set to invoke the Defense Production Act to preempt state laws and allow Sable Offshore Crop to restart oil production in California.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text:
The secret to why fractal graphene is superior is all about reach.

Imagine you are trying to reinforce a large bowl of jello (the polymer) using flat sheets of paper (standard graphene) versus a tumbleweed (fractal graphene).

The "Tangle" Factor:
Standard graphene is like a stack of papers; it’s strong, but it’s hard to mix evenly. It tends to clump together. Fractal graphene grows in a "tree-branch" or "snowflake" shape. Because it has all those branches, it naturally tangles into the polymer chains like a burr sticking to a sweater. This creates a much stronger physical grip.

The "Web" Efficiency:
To make a plastic conductive, the graphene pieces have to touch each other to let electrons flow. With flat sheets, you need a lot of material to bridge the gaps. With a fractal structure, those long, spindly arms reach out in every direction. It creates a complete electrical web using way less material, keeping the polymer light and flexible while making it as conductive as metal.

Surface Area:
Fractal shapes have a massive amount of "skin" (surface area) compared to their size. Every "branch" provides a new spot for the polymer to grab onto. It’s like the difference between trying to hold onto a smooth pole versus a climbing wall with thousands of handholds.
In short: while regular graphene is a strong material, fractal graphene is a better network, turning the entire polymer into a single, reinforced "super-material."
posts.author: BambroughKevin
